title: Read cap measures the pretty/line-counted form while --json emits compact — conservative over-refusal + byte-vs-line message imprecision (pre-existing, cross-channel)
description: Surfaced by the FGAP-015 re-audit (NOT introduced by it). truncateHead measures the pretty JSON.stringify(x, null, 2) byte size AND a 2000-line dimension; the CLI --json envelope emits the COMPACT JSON.stringify(x). So a value whose compact emitted form is well under 50KB but whose pretty form exceeds 50KB, or which exceeds 2000 lines, fails closed under --json even though the emitted compact payload would fit. This is conservative — it over-refuses, never under-protects (cannot leak) — and is inherited truncateHead behavior the {read} channel already exhibits; FGAP-015 applied the same primitive to the {json} boundary cap consistently. Secondary: the REFUSAL message reads 'N bytes, over the 50KB cap' even when the actual trigger is the 2000-line dimension, so the stated reason can be cosmetically inaccurate.

evidence.reference: truncateHead measures pretty-serialized bytes + DEFAULT_MAX_LINES=2000; the cap dimension that fires is not surfaced in the refusal text
boundedJsonOutput (defined here) measures the pretty form; the CLI --json envelope emits compact JSON.stringify({ok,op,output}) at its cli.ts:1135-1136 call site -> measurement form != emission form
structureForRead/renderReadText measure the pretty content the same way — the {read} channel has the same pre-existing over-refusal

impact: A legitimate read whose compact --json payload would fit under the cap can be refused (functional over-restriction for machine --json consumers); the refusal message can misstate bytes-vs-lines as the trigger. Safe (never leaks), low severity, but a real ergonomic imprecision spanning the cap primitive and both output channels.
proposed_resolution: Decide the cap's measurement basis per surface: for --json, measure the EMITTED compact form (so the cap reflects what actually ships); for text/Pi-tool, the pretty form is what the agent reads, so measuring pretty is right there. Surface the firing dimension (bytes vs lines) in the REFUSAL message. Its own canonical-pipeline arc; distinct from FGAP-015's channel-bypass.
